name: Adam Hattersley
subject: Hurricane Dorian
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hattersley for Congress
body:
Hi Peter,

I know it’s been a while since you last heard from me, but with our state still under threat from Hurricane Dorian, I wanted to quickly check in with you.

While Dorian’s timing and path continues to change, I hope that you and your family will be able to stay dry and safe over the next few days.

BUT if you are fortunate enough to not be in Dorian’s path, I hope you’ll take a moment to pay it forward, Peter.

From the Bahamas to our Atlantic coastline, families hit by this storm are suffering through devastating damage. If you can, I hope you’ll take a moment to give what you can to assist in emergency relief efforts.

Give to the American Red Cross >> https://www.redcross.org/donate/cm/weathercomibm-emp.html/

Give aid to the Bahamas >> https://impact.publicgood.com/campaign/3267a266-ee73-4daa-b7fe-778d036c5e6a?location=&title=Here%27s%20how%20you%20can%20help%20Hurricane%20Dorian%20victims%20&utm_content=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.cnn.com%2F2019%2F09%2F03%2Fus%2Fiyw-how-to-help-hurricane-dorian-victims-trnd%2Findex.html&utm_medium=button&utm_source=cnn

Thank you and please stay safe,
Adam

body:
We will fight.
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802df9/4bef7a87/68f86a00/287f1cc3/2179490613/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Friend -
For those of you who don't know Leonard Leo, he's the founder of the Federalist Society who wrote Trump's short list for Supreme Court nominees.
He's the man who gave us Neil Gorsuch and Brett Kavanaugh - and he's not stopping there. Here's what Leonard Leo just said about the possibility of another Trump nominee on the Supreme Court:
_" Susan Collins will have to just be comfortable with the state of play."_
Leo is already counting on Susan Collins to fall in line and vote for whichever far-right extremist Trump puts forward for the Supreme Court.
And why wouldn't he? That's exactly what she did with Gorsuch AND Kavanaugh!
**Susan Collins can 't be trusted to protect the needs of Mainers, or women. It's time for someone who will:**
[**Make a $3 contribution to defeat Susan Collins and elect Sara Gideon to the U.S. Senate. This is important.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802df9/4bef7a87/68f86a00/287f1cc2/2179490613/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA5MDQtYSJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-september?refcode=em190904-a")
Donald Trump and Leonard Leo want to stack the Supreme Court with anti-choice justices so they could have the chance to overturn _Roe v. Wade_ for good. Having Susan Collins' vote in the Senate is an important part of their plan.
[**We won 't let them turn back the clock on women's rights. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: USMCA Good Deal for Union Workers
body:
"When members of Congress return from their August recess, they’ll face one of the most important decisions of their political careers." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d1b1444cfb&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **USMCA Good Deal for Union Workers**

“When members of Congress return from their August recess, they’ll face one of the most important decisions of their political careers — whether to ratify President Trump’s new trade deal with Canada and Mexico,” Deena Flinchum writes in the _Pittsburgh Tribune-Review_.

The United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA) is a better deal in every sense than NAFTA, the outdated pact that currently governs North American trade. “USMCA would strengthen labor protections, create jobs and discourage U.S. firms from outsourcing work abroad,” Flinchum explains.

“NAFTA enabled giant corporations to outsource jobs . . . Unions have railed against NAFTA for three decades. Now, their allies in Congress finally have an opportunity to scrap that deal and replace it with a new agreement that protects American workers.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=142149b208&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “White House adviser Ivanka Trump helped to launch a U.S.-funded initiative supporting women entrepreneurs in Colombia on Tuesday as part of her three-nation tour of South America,” Dave Boyer reports for [_The Washington Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=935889202a&e=3ee4985eea). “Ms. Trump said the new Academy of Women Entrepreneurs will help to reduce barriers to women’s participation in the economy” and “equip women with the practical skills they need to create sustainable businesses and to participate more fully in the global economy.”
---
|
---
| “James Comey, the former FBI director who oversaw the investigation into Hillary Clinton’s unlawful handling of official emails, has been chastised by the Justice Department for his improper handling of official memos. The irony is so thick that it’s hard not to laugh,” the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=63ad19c6a9&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. “But there’s a serious problem here: Comey’s and Clinton’s actions reflect an arrogance of power and a gross disregard for the rule of law. The work that public servants produce belongs to the public. It doesn’t belong in one’s study at home or on one’s homebrewed server.”
---
|
---
| “Funny, but I didn’t find much about the criticism of former FBI director James Comey on The New York Times website last Friday. That was the day after the Justice Department’s internal investigator, Michael Horowitz, blasted Comey for being deceitful about memos he leaked, through a friend, to The Times,” John Crudele writes in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2efec28dcb&e=3ee4985eea). It’s “embarrassing when the public gets to see how news stories come about.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=080b0b0973&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e774abfeb8&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=53fd6173b7)
---

body:
Will you sign your name and publicly endorse me to be New Mexico's next Democratic Senator before midnight? It would mean so much to me. >>
---
| | I wanted to ask you personally: [_**Will you sign your name and publicly endorse me to be New Mexico 's next Democratic Senator before midnight? It would mean so much to me. >> **_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4be/18e32a7f/528abcc7/7bee7f18/1136215278/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/endorse-benray?source=20190904_em_pet_b1_lujan_all_rs") I'm so proud of the fights we won together last year. I led the charge to take back the House from Republicans' dangerous grasp -- and Speaker Nancy Pelosi and I have spent every second in our new Majority fighting on behalf of the people who put us there. But when I look at the critical progress Mitch McConnell and his Republican Senate are blocking us from making -- I feel compelled to step up. **That 's why I'm running for Senate -- and why the endorsement of real, hardworking families like yours means so much to me.** [_**Over the next few weeks, I 'll publicly release a list of Americans who have lent their endorsement to my Senate campaign. I would love for you to be one of them. >>**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4be/18e32a7f/528abcc7/7bee7f19/1136215278/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "http://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/endorse-benray?source=20190904_em_pet_b1_lujan_all_rs") Thank you so much for your support. -Ben Ray Lujan

|

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name to support our new ten-year plan to achieve 100% clean energy for America:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Governor Jay Inslee challenged all Americans to confront the urgency of the climate crisis bearing down upon us. I'm embracing that challenge. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852935a/45c30650/13ecacf7e/66869f06/2674852869/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
| **Tune in tonight to hear Elizabeth talk about our plans to fight climate change. She'll be onstage at CNN's Climate Crisis town hall around 9:20 p.m. ET.**
---

As a presidential candidate, my friend Governor Jay Inslee challenged all Americans to confront the urgency of the climate crisis bearing down upon us.

He provided bold, thoughtful, and detailed ideas for how to get us where we need to go. While his presidential campaign may be over, his ideas should remain at the center of this race.

One of the most important of these ideas is the urgent need to decarbonize key sectors of our economy.

**Now, I'm embracing that goal and committing to adopt and build on Governor Inslee's ten-year action plan to achieve 100% clean energy for America by decarbonizing our electricity, our vehicles, and our buildings. And I'm challenging every other candidate for president to do the same.**

**[If you're in this fight all the way, add your name to support our plan for 100% clean energy in ten years.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852935a/45c30650/13ecacf7e/66869f09/2674852869/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUowNVBfUkxUX0VOVl9FXzIwMTkwOTA0X1YxUzNfUF9UMiJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-clean-energy-plan?source=EMJ05P_RLT_ENV_E_20190904_V1S3_P_T2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

[**SIGN MY NAME**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852935a/45c30650/13ecacf7e/66869f09/2674852869/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUowNVBfUkxUX0VOVl9FXzIwMTkwOTA0X1YxUzNfUF9UMiJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-clean-energy-plan?source=EMJ05P_RLT_ENV_E_20190904_V1S3_P_T2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---

Put together, our electricity, vehicle, and building sectors are responsible for nearly 70 percent of all U.S. carbon emissions -- so this plan will make a real difference to combat climate change.

We have the technology today to start to construct a cleaner grid, modernize our auto industry, and green our communities, and my Green Apollo program will fund the research and develop the technology we need to go the final mile. I'm committing $1 trillion to subsidize the consumer transition to clean and renewable electricity, zero emission vehicles, and green products for commercial and residential buildings.

**And in a Warren administration, there won't be a tension between transitioning to a green economy and creating good, middle-class, union jobs. We'll do both.** And we won't leave behind communities of color and others who've been systematically excluded from our fossil fuel economy and hit worst by climate change and pollution.

This is the promise of the Green New Deal. We can lift up frontline communities, empower workers, and tackle climate change -- all at the same time.

As president, I'll work to achieve **100% clean, renewable and zero-emission energy in electricity generation by 2035.**

To get there, we'll set high standards for utilities nationwide, and then tighten them over time. We'll overhaul the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission so its mission is reducing greenhouse gas pollution and so it works for our communities, not fossil fuel interests. We'll use federal purchasing power and subsidies to speed up the transition, and expand interstate and regional coordination.

And by 2030, we'll achieve **zero emissions for all new light-duty passenger vehicles, medium-duty trucks, and buses.**

How? By setting ambitious standards for fuels and emissions, modernizing the automotive manufacturing base, developing needed infrastructure, and boosting consumer demand -- including extending tax credits for purchasing zero-emissions vehicles and creating a "Clean Cars for Clunkers" program.

As president, I'll also take immediate action to achieve **zero-carbon pollution from all new commercial and residential buildings by the end of my second term in 2028.**

**To make that happen, we will adopt bold standards for construction, use federal buying power to drive change, encourage private capital investments, and incentivize retrofits of existing building stock.**

We've woven in plans to fight climate change throughout our policy proposals on this campaign. From boosting green manufacturing and supporting sustainable agriculture to fighting corruption to shake the fossil fuel lobby's grip off Washington, we've got a plan for that.

If we work together to make smart investments in our clean energy future, we will grow our economy, improve our health, and reduce structural inequalities embedded in our existing fossil fuel system.

The task before us is monumental, and it is urgent. But I'm confident that America has the tools, the know-how, and the will to make it happen.

**[As president, I will take bold action to confront the climate crisis, starting on day one. The future of our planet depends on it, and we have no more time to waste. But we need a grassroots movement to make real change. clean_body: Governor Jay Inslee challenged all Americans to confront the urgency of the climate crisis bearing down upon us. I'm embracing that challenge. | | | "" | Tune in tonight to hear Elizabeth talk about our plans to fight climate change. She'll be onstage at CNN's Climate Crisis town hall around 9:20 p.m. ET. As a presidential candidate, my friend Governor Jay Inslee challenged all Americans to confront the urgency of the climate crisis bearing down upon us. He provided bold, thoughtful, and detailed ideas for how to get us where we need to go. While his presidential campaign may be over, his ideas should remain at the center of this race. One of the most important of these ideas is the urgent need to decarbonize key sectors of our economy. Now, I'm embracing that goal and committing to adopt and build on Governor Inslee's ten-year action plan to achieve 100% clean energy for America by decarbonizing our electricity, our vehicles, and our buildings. And I'm challenging every other candidate for president to do the same. "" "" Put together, our electricity, vehicle, and building sectors are responsible for nearly 70 percent of all U.S. carbon emissions -- so this plan will make a real difference to combat climate change. We have the technology today to start to construct a cleaner grid, modernize our auto industry, and green our communities, and my Green Apollo program will fund the research and develop the technology we need to go the final mile. I'm committing $1 trillion to subsidize the consumer transition to clean and renewable electricity, zero emission vehicles, and green products for commercial and residential buildings. And in a Warren administration, there won't be a tension between transitioning to a green economy and creating good, middle-class, union jobs. We'll do both. And we won't leave behind communities of color and others who've been systematically excluded from our fossil fuel economy and hit worst by climate change and pollution. This is the promise of the Green New Deal. We can lift up frontline communities, empower workers, and tackle climate change -- all at the same time. As president, I'll work to achieve 100% clean, renewable and zero-emission energy in electricity generation by 2035. To get there, we'll set high standards for utilities nationwide, and then tighten them over time. We'll overhaul the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission so its mission is reducing greenhouse gas pollution and so it works for our communities, not fossil fuel interests. We'll use federal purchasing power and subsidies to speed up the transition, and expand interstate and regional coordination. And by 2030, we'll achieve zero emissions for all new light-duty passenger vehicles, medium-duty trucks, and buses. How? By setting ambitious standards for fuels and emissions, modernizing the automotive manufacturing base, developing needed infrastructure, and boosting consumer demand -- including extending tax credits for purchasing zero-emissions vehicles and creating a "Clean Cars for Clunkers" program. As president, I'll also take immediate action to achieve zero-carbon pollution from all new commercial and residential buildings by the end of my second term in 2028. To make that happen, we will adopt bold standards for construction, use federal buying power to drive change, encourage private capital investments, and incentivize retrofits of existing building stock. We've woven in plans to fight climate change throughout our policy proposals on this campaign. From boosting green manufacturing and supporting sustainable agriculture to fighting corruption to shake the fossil fuel lobby's grip off Washington, we've got a plan for that. If we work together to make smart investments in our clean energy future, we will grow our economy, improve our health, and reduce structural inequalities embedded in our existing fossil fuel system. The task before us is monumental, and it is urgent. But I'm confident that America has the tools, the know-how, and the will to make it happen. "" Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth "" Elizabeth doesn't accept contributions from PACs of any kind or federally registered lobbyists. This grassroots movement is powered by supporters like you. | | I just got the news:

Because of Mitch McConnell, our country's electoral system has been thrown into disarray.

The Federal Election Commission -- critically tasked with protecting the integrity of our elections from interference -- is powerless.

[**I need 1,000 signatures on my emergency petition before midnight condemning McConnell 's blatant attack on our elections. We can't let him cheat his way into a Republican Senate. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c7/232dfd1/81844e1/62a1ca5b/4270786848/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-election-security-petition?source=20190904_em_pet_b1_baer_all")

As of right now:

McConnell refuses to pass a single bill to stop foreign interference in the 2020 election, even while the House passed HR1, a bill full of election security reforms.

Campaign corruption and fraudulent financial activity will go unchecked.

Election laws that protect fairness in our democracy have been thrown out.

Wealthy donors and special interests are free to entirely influence our elections.

Point blank: Mitch McConnell has singlehandedly put our electoral system in jeopardy.

**Add your name to my emergency petition condemning Mitch McConnell for attacking the security of the 2020 election:**

[SIGN ON >>](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c7/232dfd1/81844e1/62a1ca5b/4270786848/VEsE/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-election-security-petition?source=20190904_em_pet_b1_baer_all")

body:
Let's keep Kansas red | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/e58d9faeada9/were-running-for-congress?e=1050d051da)
---
| |
---
| | **Kansas State Treasurer Jake LaTurner Announces Candidacy for U.S. Congress** I want you to be the first to know some exciting news that we are announcing today.

After careful consideration and lots of prayer, Suzanne and I have made the decision to run for the 2nd Congressional District. I am humbled by the kind, [public encouragement Governor Jeff Colyer gave last week](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=0b5cc2116b&e=1050d051da) and encouraged that Dave Murfin and Ivan Crossland have stepped up to co-chair this campaign for Congress.

Suzanne and I live in Topeka and grew up in Southeast Kansas. We care deeply about the Second District, our great State, and the future of this country. Second District residents deserve a solid conservative fighter they can count on.

I wake up every day appalled at the news coming from the Democrats in Washington, DC. They want to transform America into a country that we would not recognize. The 2020 election is a critical one for our state and nation. We must nominate a Republican that can win the general election and fight for our conservative principles.

I have outlined my seven non-negotiable promises in my [Contract with Kansas](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=524cf1d18a&e=1050d051da), including protecting life and the second amendment, securing the border, and seeking term limits for elected officials.

As we kick off this campaign, we could really use your support. Keeping the 2nd District seat in Republican hands is vital if we want our best shot at protecting conservative values in Washington. [_You can help make that happen by making a contribution to my campaign for Congress **here**._](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=acdd4827e3&e=1050d051da)

Thank you again for your incredible support. Let's keep Kansas red!

Yours in the fight!
Jake LaTurner P.S. Your contribution to this effort is vital. [**Please make your most generous contribution today!**](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=9c596b8d6e&e=1050d051da)

body:
---
Peter,

I'm going to ask you to consider a contribution to Dan McCready for Congress in a moment, but first, let me tell you why it's so important.

[_**Dan is facing an extremely close special election in six days. This race has been rated as a toss-up by Cook Political Report, and most polling shows the race within the margin of error.**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/bK8OKv79I6UnGMAXi0fFlyNbPQXNh2o6V93OJy8irRY=/DMv1dViSz88A3hkmIZIpmFfJgRWu0HO9UNPFrulFHuk=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190904-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=5,10,20,50,100,250)

Dan McCready is a veteran, an entrepreneur, and my friend. Dan's commitment to service began when he joined the Marine Corps in the years following 9/11. After his service, he returned to North Carolina where he started a solar energy company to expand the use of renewable energy across his state. Dan is a father of four and a fighter. He's working to ensure the voices of those whose votes were stolen in 2018 count in this special election, and every day he represents them in Congress. Dan believes firmly in our country and will put it ahead of partisan interests. And like me, he does not take corporate PAC donations.

His opponent, on the other hand, has a history of hate that even former Republicans have said must be opposed. Dan Bishop is an investor in a social media platform favored by Neo-Nazis and has openly espoused discrimination against the LGBT community.

This race was nearly stolen in 2018. Despite election fraud, the margin was still a mere 905 votes. This is a winnable race, but Dan McCready needs our help.

[_**Will you consider a contribution today to help him get over the finish line and win this important race next Tuesday?**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/bK8OKv79I6UnGMAXi0fFlyNbPQXNh2o6V93OJy8irRY=/DMv1dViSz88A3hkmIZIpmFfJgRWu0HO9UNPFrulFHuk=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190904-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=5,10,20,50,100,250) | | | [ DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/bK8OKv79I6UnGMAXi0fFlyNbPQXNh2o6V93OJy8irRY=/DMv1dViSz88A3hkmIZIpmFfJgRWu0HO9UNPFrulFHuk=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190904-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=5,10,20,50,100,250)

body:
President Obama's Affordable Care Act has been a _huge success_ in providing affordable healthcare access to millions of Americans.

**Unfortunately, the Trump Administration is now trying to undermine one of the most popular aspects of Obamacare by allowing states to sell health insurance plans that do not adequately protect Americans with preexisting conditions.**

[**Tell Congress: Keep Protections for Americans with Preexisting Conditions -- SIGN ON »**](https://act.myngp.com/el/bD_gL38Qwp5s2PwSfY6C5Q4AXJDzmc4Pps1rg-GWZi4=/ykGfd9PCPOOqPvmRCB6eqGSjl1kZ9lNFfkc1IEvMnQ0=?code=stanton&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fcampaignlaunch2020%3Frefcode%3Dx_preexistingconditionsJA%26amount%3D25%26recurring%3Dauto)

Without protections for preexisting conditions, Americans may not have access to the care they need.

name: Team Beto
subject: Outrageous misinformation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
Tech companies can no longer turn a blind eye to right-wing operatives using their platforms to spread damaging misinformation.
#
We need to talk about tech.
This past weekend, a right-wing bot network spread the **_completely false_** rumor that the Odessa shooter had a Beto sticker on his car.
* On Twitter, we saw _thousands_ of tweets echo this conspiracy theory, some of which received **_tens of thousands_** of retweets.

* On Facebook, a single post containing this misinformation received more than **_34,000_** shares.

* And, on Google, this malicious conspiracy theory became the **_second-highest-trending_** search query related to Beto for the preceding week.

Tech companies can no longer turn a blind eye to right-wing operatives using their platforms to spread damaging misinformation. [As president, Beto will push for more accountability for tech companies. Will you kick in $3 and support our efforts to fight back today?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31307?refcode=ema20190904-53181&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=53181%2E444211%2Ekaqnbh)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31307?refcode=ema20190904-53181&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=53181%2E444211%2Ekaqnbh)**
Campaigns like ours are almost entirely powerless to stop misinformation like this from spreading.
Sure, we can send out corrections, but only a small fragment of the people exposed to the misinformation will ever see those.
More to the point, **the responsibility for stopping misinformation**** _can’t_****rest on the victims**.
Without swift action from Twitter, Facebook, and Google -- the companies who own the platforms that the right uses to spread misinformation -- dangerous, harmful conspiracy theories will continue to propagate, unchecked.
It _has_ to fall to the tech companies to police their own platforms and to stop the spread of misinformation before tens of thousands of people are exposed to malicious conspiracy theories.
But, if tech companies won’t do it on their own, President Beto O’Rourke will make them do it. [You can join our efforts to hold tech accountable by contributing $3 to our campaign today.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31307?refcode=ema20190904-53181&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=53181%2E444211%2Ekaqnbh)
And remember -- don’t believe everything you read on the internet.
Thanks!
-Team Beto

clean_body: Tech companies can no longer turn a blind eye to right-wing operatives using their platforms to spread damaging misinformation. We need to talk about tech. This past weekend, a right-wing bot network spread the completely false rumor that the Odessa shooter had a Beto sticker on his car. On Twitter, we saw thousands of tweets echo this conspiracy theory, some of which received tens of thousands of retweets. On Facebook, a single post containing this misinformation received more than 34,000 shares. And, on Google, this malicious conspiracy theory became the second-highest-trending search query related to Beto for the preceding week. Tech companies can no longer turn a blind eye to right-wing operatives using their platforms to spread damaging misinformation. >
**Boosting green manufacturing**
Elizabeth's green manufacturing plan would invest $2 trillion over the next ten years in green research, manufacturing, and exporting -- creating more than a million good jobs at home and helping achieve the ambitious targets of the Green New Deal.

**Fighting corruption in Washington**
To reduce the influence of Big Oil on our government, Elizabeth has proposed the biggest anti-corruption reform package since Watergate -- ending lobbying as we know it. She also supports eliminating fossil fuel subsidies and using that money to invest in clean and renewable energy and infrastructure instead.

**Protecting public lands**
On her first day as president, Elizabeth will sign an executive order that says no more drilling -- a total moratorium on all new fossil fuel leases for drilling offshore and on public lands. She will fight to restore and expand protections to national monuments targeted by the Trump administration, and she'll fully fund our public land management agencies.

**Supporting family farmers and sustainable agriculture**
Elizabeth's new farm economy plan will radically expand a tried-and-true program -- the Conservation Stewardship Program -- that funds farmers transitioning to sustainable practices, so that every farmer who wants to use their land to fight climate change can do so.

**Investing in green infrastructure**
Elizabeth knows we should reject new fossil fuel infrastructure and projects that will emit greenhouse gases for decades to come. As president, she'll subject new infrastructure projects to a climate test, and she'll revoke permits for pipelines like Keystone XL.

**Setting and living up to bold international standards to reduce carbon emissions**
Elizabeth will return the United States to the Paris Climate Accord. Her Green Marshall Plan will provide American-made clean energy technology to countries that need it most and will commit $100 billion over ten years to offer discounts to countries hardest hit by the climate crisis, or as an incentive for regulatory changes that further reduce emissions.

**Leveraging trade to raise environmental standards around the world**
Under Elizabeth's trade plan, countries will be required to join the Paris Climate Agreement and eliminate domestic fuel subsidies as a precondition for entering trade negotiations with the United States, and she'll impose a border carbon adjustment on carbon-intensive goods.

**Addressing how climate change disproportionately affects Native people**
Elizabeth's full plan to honor and empower Tribal Nations and indigenous peoples will involve ending the practice of placing the interests of companies that want to exploit our environment ahead of the interests of Native people who want to preserve sacred sites.

**Fighting for clean air and water and protecting vulnerable communities**
Elizabeth will restore the Obama-era environmental protections that safeguard the air we breathe and the water we drink. Her new clean energy plan will address environmental injustice and strengthen legal protections for communities that have been disproportionately threatened by accelerating climate change.

The climate crisis is a massive threat to all of us. We can solve it -- but it's going to take a massive grassroots movement. And it's going to take a president who is committed to big, structural change. Elizabeth is in this fight all the way.

**[But she can't do it alone. He’s started unwinnable trade wars; he’s broken decades of precedent and interfered with the independence of the Federal Reserve; he’s used Twitter to tank markets at his whim; AND _**he introduced some of the most fiscally irresponsible policies of any Republican President in American history.**_ But the one thing this article makes perfectly clear is that things are culminating into a potential crisis — a financial crisis of President Trump’s own making — and it’s one that makes him vulnerable in 2020. We believe that when push comes to shove, American voters know _**the LAST person they want leading their country through a financial crisis is Donald J. Trump** _— and that gives us a chance to show them Bill’s leadership and his vision for returning fiscal sanity back to Washington. But if we want voters to hear our message, we’re going to need your help. But if we want voters to hear our message, we’re going to need your help. Thanks for being a part of history, — Team Weld | | Paid for by Weld 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States

subject: President Trump gives an update on Hurricane Dorian
body:
In the Oval Office today, President Donald J. Trump received a briefing on the ongoing Government-wide response to Hurricane Dorian. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c7eaa6e749&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • September 4, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump gives an update on Hurricane Dorian**

**In the Oval Office today,** President Donald J. Trump received a briefing on the ongoing Government-wide response to Hurricane Dorian. He also shared some important next steps the Administration will be taking to support local relief efforts.

**Watch:** [President Trump gives an update on Hurricane Dorian](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6402a7a4fb&e=3ee4985eea)

**“It looks like Florida** is going to be in fantastic shape by comparison to what we thought,” the President said. “It’s starting to move up along the coast,” and “we’re very well prepared” should the storm rapidly turn west.

**While Hurricane Dorian** moves along the Eastern seaboard, President Trump is asking all residents in Georgia, South Carolina, and North Carolina to heed warnings from their local officials.

**Following the storm’s** devastating impact on the Bahamas, the President announced that incredible members of our U.S. Coast Guard are assisting with rescue efforts there now. Additional humanitarian assistance will be on the way, as well.

[Stay prepared: Up-to-date FEMA resources and information on Hurricane Dorian](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8fc9651fe&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬****More:**[President Trump says relief efforts are underway in the Bahamas](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=32b4149a55&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the Day: Vice President Pence visits Ireland**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd104a8b39&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Yesterday,** Vice President Mike Pence visited Dublin, where he met with Ireland’s head of state, Taoiseach Leo Varadkar. At the historic Farmleigh House, the two leaders underscored the unwavering strength of the American–Irish partnership.

**“It is a dynamic** economic relationship between the United States and Ireland,” the Vice President said at a joint press conference. “And it’s only growing stronger by the day.”

**This particular trip** carried some strong family ties for Vice President Pence. As he joked, the Taoiseach’s invitation came with “strings attached”—that the Vice President must bring his mother Nancy, a first-generation Irish-American, along with him.

**In 1923,** Vice President Pence’s grandfather first came to America from Ireland’s shores. After his grandfather passed in 1981, Mike Pence traveled to Ireland for the first time to see the land where his ancestors grew up.

**“Our family cherishes** our Irish heritage,” the Vice President said, and during that month-and-a-half trip, “I came to realize that I carry Ireland with me wherever I go—just like more than 30 million Americans who trace their heritage to the Emerald Isle.”
---
|
---
|
# **JUST ANNOUNCED: Nearly $2 billion in new funding to combat America’s opioid crisis**

**Today,** President Trump announced that $1.8 billion in new grant money is on its way to help local communities fight opioid addiction.

**“These funds** will be delivered to the communities where the help is most needed,” the President said. We’re well on our way “to building a drug-free future for our nation.”

**More than****$900 million** of that money will be spread across all 50 states as flexible funds to support prevention, treatment, and recovery services that meet local needs. Another $900 million will help track overdose data in a timely and comprehensive way, helping to develop strategies that will connect suffering Americans with proper treatment.

[See how President Trump is fighting to end America’s opioid crisis.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=54388d7cf4&e=3ee4985eea)

**Share your story:** [The “Crisis Next Door” affects Americans of all backgrounds](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ac12c2ff8f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
| **President Trump speaks with Bahamian Prime Minister Hubert Minnis, expressing his condolences for the loss of life and catastrophic damage in parts of the Bahamas. The President also confirmed the United States’ commitment to provide prompt humanitarian assistance to the affected people and communities of the Bahamas.** | September 4, 2019
